# Build Young

> A live, hands-on program for teenagers (ages 13–18) that teaches them to build economic value. Tagline: "Raising builders, not consumers." Students first build something people will pay for — that becomes their income — then learn to manage what they earn. In an AI world, the edge isn't a degree; it's what you can build.

## About
Build Young is an online program founded by Sunil Garg that teaches teens to build economic value. First they build something other people will pay for (using a skill and AI to create real value) — and that becomes their income; then they learn to manage what they earn: business taxes, saving, investing so it compounds, big purchases, and budgeting. It runs as a 12-week course (90-minute weekly sessions) plus 6 monthly check-ins, delivered live over Zoom, inside an interactive simulation where income is earned by the student's build. The program is financial education, not licensed financial advice; all money in the simulation is simulated.

## Who it's for
Teens ages 13 and up, in two tracks:
- Middle School cohort — $899
- High School cohort — $899

## What students learn
Act 1 — Build (Weeks 1–6):
- Find a real problem people will pay to solve
- Shape it into a product or service and make a first version with AI as a tool
- Launch, get first customers, price it, and grow what's working — your build becomes your income
Act 2 — Manage what you've earned (Weeks 7–12):
- Business finances: self-employment taxes and paying yourself
- Saving, investing, diversification, and compounding
- How macro forces (inflation, interest rates, recessions) move markets
- Big purchases: buying and financing a home and a car; good vs. bad debt
- Budgeting and handling financial surprises
Then 6 monthly check-ins managing the business and portfolio they built.

## Enrolling
Families can book a free 15-minute call with the founder before enrolling. A prorated refund is available through the end of Act 1 (first 3 weeks).
